revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:加茂神社の稚児舞]]


'''Chigo Dance of Kamo Shrine''' (加茂神社の稚児舞) is a traditional folk performing art passed down at the {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Kamo Shrine (Imizu)|lt=Shimomura Kamo Shrine|ja|加茂神社 (射水市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimomura Kamo Shrine|qq=}} in Kamo Nakabu, {{ill|Imizu|en|Imizu|qid=Q851411|lt=Imizu City|1=Imizu}}, Toyama Prefecture. It has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}}.
== Overview ==
Shimomura Kamo Shrine was established on the former manor of the {{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|Shimogamo Shrine|qid=Q701620|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} in Kyoto, known as the Kurakaki Manor. The shrine's {{ill|Chigo Dance|ja|稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance|qq=}} is said to have been transmitted from Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine during the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Performed on September 4th during the autumn festival, the tradition includes a ritual called "Renkō," where four {{ill|Chigo|ja|稚児|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo|qq=}} (young boys) are carried on adults' shoulders and paraded around the 2-kilometer route through the village. This is followed by performances of nine dances, including the "Hoko no Mai" (Dance of the Halberd), "Rinka" (Forest Song), "Kochō no Mai" (Butterfly Dance), and "Ama no Mai" (Dance of Heaven), staged on a temporary platform in front of the shrine's worship hall. Originally, there were dd dances.<ref>North Japan ddspaper, "Suiki-iki Toyama's Festivals: Chigo Dance of Shimomura Kamo Shrine - Sustained by Local Boys," September 18, 2017, p. 9.</ref>
On January 1, 1965, the dance was designated as a Toyama Prefecture Intangible Folk Cultural Property.<ref>Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato in 100 Years, published November 27, 1993, Kyodo Shuppan, p. 164.</ref> It was selected as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property Recommended for Measures such as Recording|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property Recommended for Measures such as Recording|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財}} on June 8, 1970. Subsequently, in 1982, along with dances such as {{ill|Myō no Chigo Dance|ja|明日の稚児舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myō no Chigo Dance|1=Ashita no Chigo Mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512132}} and {{ill|Chigo Dance of Kumano Shrine (Toyama City)|ja|熊野神社の稚児舞 (富山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigo Dance of Kumano Shrine (Toyama City)|1=Kumano Shrine Chigo-mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568967}}, it was collectively designated as part of the "Etchū Chigo Dance" (越中の稚児舞) as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property.<ref>Visual History of Takaoka, Himi, and Shinminato in 100 Years, published November 27, 1993, Kyodo Shuppan, p. 165.</ref> Additionally, in 2006, it was selected as part of the "100 Cultural Heritage Selections of Toyama: Festival Edition."
On October 25, 2015, during the 35th {{ill|National Ocean Afforestation Festival|ja|全国豊かな海づくり大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Ocean Afforestation Festival|1=The National Convention for the Development of an Abundantly Productive Sea|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11389560}}, the "Kochō no Mai" was performed in the presence of the Emperor and Empress at the maritime welcome and release ceremony held at Shinminato’s {{ill|Kaiōmaru Park|ja|海王丸パーク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiōmaru Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 海王丸 (初代)|1=Kaiwo Maru (1930)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q365055}}.<ref>North Japan ddspaper, "35th National Ocean Afforestation Festival - A Toyama Welcome for Their Majesties," October 26, 2015, pp. 16–17.</ref>
